description: Fetch historical S&P 500 constituents from Wikipedia by scraping current list and historical changes, then reconstructing backwards chronologically
author: Claude

steps.name: fetch_wikipedia_page
steps.description: Fetch the Wikipedia page containing S&P 500 constituent data

headers.User-Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/91.0.4472.124 Safari/537.36

error_handling.on_403: CRITICAL - Wikipedia blocks requests without User-Agent. Ensure header is set.

steps.name: parse_current_constituents
steps.description: Extract current S&P 500 constituents from the first HTML table on the page

error_handling.on_missing_table: CRITICAL - Wikipedia page structure may have changed
error_handling.on_missing_column: Check for column name variations (Symbol, Ticker, etc.)

steps.name: parse_changes_table
steps.description: Extract historical changes (additions/removals) from the second HTML table

error_handling.on_multiindex_columns: Flatten MultiIndex by joining with underscore
error_handling.on_invalid_date: Use errors='coerce' to set invalid dates to NaT, then filter out

data_processing.description: Convert MultiIndex columns to single-level by joining tuples
Extract ticker symbol by splitting on '(' and taking first part (removes company name)
Remove rows where Date is NaT (not a valid timestamp)

steps.implementation: pd.read_html(html_content)[1] with MultiIndex handling and date parsing

steps.name: reconstruct_historical
steps.description: Reconstruct historical constituent lists by starting from current list and reversing changes backwards through time

algorithm_details.name: Backwards Reconstruction Algorithm
steps: Generate list of all months between start_date and end_date
Start with the most recent month using current_tickers as baseline
For each month going backwards in time:
- Find all changes that occurred AFTER this month's end date
* If ticker was ADDED in future → REMOVE it from current month
* If ticker was REMOVED in future → ADD it back to current month
- Store the resulting constituent list for this month
Return dictionary mapping month strings (YYYY-MM) to sorted ticker lists
algorithm_details.rationale: Wikipedia provides current state + change log. Easier to reconstruct backwards than forwards.
algorithm_details.complexity: O(M * C) where M = months, C = changes

error_handling.on_missing_ticker: Log warning but continue (historical data may be incomplete)

steps.name: save_output
steps.description: Save the reconstructed historical constituents to a JSON file

schema.description: Dictionary mapping month strings to lists of ticker symbols
schema.value_format: array of uppercase ticker symbols (1-5 characters)
schema.sorted: keys in chronological order, values in alphabetical order

packages.name: pandas
packages.purpose: HTML table parsing and data manipulation

packages.name: requests
packages.purpose: HTTP requests to fetch Wikipedia page

packages.name: lxml
packages.purpose: HTML parsing backend for pandas (optional but recommended)

critical_fixes.cause: Wikipedia blocks requests without User-Agent header
critical_fixes.solution: Always include User-Agent header that mimics a real browser

critical_fixes.cause: Wikipedia's changes table may have MultiIndex columns from pandas.read_html
critical_fixes.solution: Flatten MultiIndex by joining tuples or converting to single-level index
critical_fixes.example: if isinstance(df.columns, pd.MultiIndex): df.columns = ['_'.join(col) for col in df.columns.values]

critical_fixes.cause: Added/Removed columns contain company names in parentheses: 'TICKER (Company Name)'
critical_fixes.solution: Split on '(' and take first part, then strip whitespace

critical_fixes.cause: Some rows in changes table may have malformed dates
critical_fixes.solution: Use pd.to_datetime with errors='coerce', then filter out NaT values

critical_fixes.cause: Pandas recommends wrapping HTML strings in StringIO for read_html
critical_fixes.solution: Wrap html_content in StringIO object (cosmetic warning, not critical)

known_limitations.limitation: Historical accuracy depends on Wikipedia data completeness
known_limitations.impact: If Wikipedia's change log is incomplete, reconstructed history may be inaccurate
known_limitations.mitigation: Cross-reference with authoritative sources like S&P Global for critical applications

known_limitations.impact: Table indices or column names might change, breaking the parser
known_limitations.mitigation: Use flexible column name matching and add validation steps

known_limitations.impact: Cannot determine exact day within a month for constituent membership
known_limitations.mitigation: Use end-of-month snapshots as the standard reference point

known_limitations.impact: If multiple tickers added/removed on same date, may need special handling
known_limitations.mitigation: Current implementation handles this by processing each change individually

validation_checks: Verify ticker count per month is between 490-510
Verify all tickers are uppercase 1-5 character strings
Verify no duplicate tickers within a month
Verify output file is valid JSON

sample_test_cases.test: Verify AAPL exists in all months
sample_test_cases.command: python -c "import json; data=json.load(open('snp500_constituents.json')); print(all('AAPL' in v for v in data.values()))"

sample_test_cases.test: Verify output has 61 months for 2020-01 to 2025-01

usage_examples.name: Custom date range
usage_examples.description: Modify start_date and end_date variables in main() function

usage_examples.name: Programmatic usage
usage_examples.code:
from fetch_sp500_constituents import *

url = 'https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_S%26P_500_companies'
headers = {'User-Agent': 'Mozilla/5.0 ...'}
html = fetch_wikipedia_page(url, headers)
current = parse_current_constituents(html)
changes = parse_changes_table(html)
result = reconstruct_backwards(current, changes, '2020-01-01', '2023-12-31')

notes: Wikipedia may block requests without User-Agent header - this is the #1 failure mode
Historical accuracy depends on Wikipedia's change log completeness
The reconstruction algorithm works backwards from current state rather than forwards from historical state
Ticker symbols are extracted from company descriptions by splitting on '(' character
Monthly snapshots represent constituents at the END of each month
S&P 500 typically has ~500-505 constituents, but can temporarily vary during transitions
For production use, consider caching the Wikipedia HTML to avoid repeated requests
For real-time applications, implement change detection to only fetch when Wikipedia updates

related_resources.name: Wikipedia S&P 500 Page
related_resources.description: Source data for current constituents and historical changes

changes: Added User-Agent header fix for 403 errors
Added comprehensive error handling and validation
